section_label: Rights — Citizenship, Rule of Law Principles, Information & Religion

variables.description: Whether the constitution provides for naturalized citizens or naturalization
Conditions under which citizenship can be revoked. Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): CITREV_1=Upon acquisition of citizenship in another country; CITREV_2=Upon conviction of a crime; CITREV_3=Upon entering employment or commission of another government; CITREV_4=Citizenship is expressly guaranteed and irrevocable; CITREV_90=Left to non-constitutional law; CITREV_96=Other; CITREV_97=Unable to Determine; CITREV_98=Not Specified

variables.description: Whether the constitution grants the government the right to deport citizens or residents
Whether the constitution restricts entry or exit of the state's borders
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes, entry restricted; 2=Yes, exit restricted; 3=Both restricted; 4=Neither; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine

variables.description: Whether the constitution contains a general statement regarding rule of law, legality, or Rechtsstaat (must be specifically mentioned, not implied; includes 'socialist legality')
Whether the constitution refers to equality before the law, equal rights, or non-discrimination
Which groups the constitution protects from discrimination or provides equality for. Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): EQUALGR_1=Gender; EQUALGR_2=Nationality; EQUALGR_3=Country of Origin; EQUALGR_4=Race; EQUALGR_5=Language; EQUALGR_6=Religion; EQUALGR_7=Sexual Orientation; EQUALGR_8=Age; EQUALGR_9=Mentally or Physically Disabled; EQUALGR_10=Color; EQUALGR_11=Creed/Beliefs; EQUALGR_12=Social Status; EQUALGR_13=Financial/Property Ownership; EQUALGR_14=Tribe/Clan; EQUALGR_15=Political Party; EQUALGR_16=Parentage; EQUALGR_90=Left to non-constitutional law; EQUALGR_96=Other; EQUALGR_97=Unable to Determine; EQUALGR_98=Not Specified

variables.description: Whether the constitution specifically restricts the rights of any particular groups. Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): RIGHTRES_1=Property Owners; RIGHTRES_2=Racial/Ethnic/Religious/Linguistic/National Minorities; RIGHTRES_3=Women; RIGHTRES_4=Non-Property Owners; RIGHTRES_5=Peasants; RIGHTRES_6=Immigrants; RIGHTRES_7=Elderly Individuals; RIGHTRES_8=Disabled Persons; RIGHTRES_90=Left to non-constitutional law; RIGHTRES_96=Other; RIGHTRES_97=Unable to Determine; RIGHTRES_98=Not Specified
variables.notes: Coded as group-specific restrictions on rights, not protections. Presence indicates discriminatory constitutional provisions.

variables.description: Whether rights provisions are binding on private parties as well as the state (must be explicitly stated, not just implied)
Whether the constitution provides for an individual right to view government files or documents under at least some conditions (right to information)
To which kinds of documents the constitution directs individuals should have access (asked only if INFOACC=1)
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Documents about oneself or family; 2=General public information; 3=Both; 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified; 99=Not Applicable

variables.description: Whether the constitution contains provisions concerning a national or official religion or church
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes, national religion specified; 2=Certain religion(s) enjoys special treatment; 3=National religion specifically forbidden (includes secular state or separation of church and state); 90=Left to non-constitutional law; 96=Other; 97=Unable to Determine; 98=Not Specified

variables.description: Whether the constitution provides for freedom of religion
variables.coding_scheme: 1=Yes; 2=No; 96=Other (includes constitutions granting freedom only to certain religions); 97=Unable to Determine

variables.description: The status of religious law in the constitutional order. Multi-response.
variables.coding_scheme: Sub-variables (each 0=not selected, 1=selected, 99=not applicable): RELLAW_1=Default law if no other law applies; RELLAW_2=A source of law; RELLAW_3=Basis, main, major, or supreme source of law; RELLAW_90=Left to non-constitutional law; RELLAW_96=Other; RELLAW_97=Unable to Determine; RELLAW_98=Not Specified
variables.notes: RELLAW_3 (religious law as supreme source) is relevant to rule of law when religious doctrine supersedes secular legal principles

variables.description: Whether law contrary to religion is void (repugnancy clause)
Whether the constitution contains an explicit decree of separation of church and state (reference to a secular state is sufficient)
